title: Lines Written On A Window[1] In The Episcopal Palace At Kilmore
content:
Resolve me this, ye happy dead,
    Who've lain some hundred years in bed,
    From every persecution free
    That in this wretched life we see;
    Would ye resume a second birth,
    And choose once more to live on earth?
meaning:
Jonathan Swift's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Lines Written On A Window[1] In The Episcopal Palace At Kilmore"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
